meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier requires shifting from passive learning to active development. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you gain an autonomous engineering partner that handles boilerplate and architecture, allowing you to focus on logic and product iteration. This workflow transforms complex coding hurdles into manageable, guided build cycles.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the student’s greatest adversary. You have the ambition to launch a SaaS, but the gulf between 'I have an idea' and 'I have a deployed product' feels like an unbridgeable canyon. Most tutorials leave you stranded in syntax errors, lacking the architectural intuition that separates a hobbyist from a professional builder.

Relief is here. We are dismantling the barrier of entry by leveraging Claude Code as your technical co-founder. By combining the rapid prototyping power of Antigravity with the intent-driven automation of Claude Code, you will stop chasing tutorials and start orchestrating software. This is not just code; it is the blueprint for your first career-defining project.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Establish a clean workspace where Claude Code can observe your project structure and manage dependencies effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project structure with a clean folder hierarchy, including /src, /tests, and a README.md file.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models to ensure your SaaS has a solid foundation for user management and core features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a user-based SaaS, including tables for profiles, subscriptions, and project data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create Prisma schema with User, Subscription, and Project models'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication
steps.description: Secure your application by setting up robust authentication, a critical requirement for any SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup Clerk or NextAuth in the project to handle user sign-in and protected routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure NextAuth with GitHub provider and protect /dashboard routes'

steps.title: Building the Core Feature
steps.description: Develop the primary value proposition of your SaaS by iterating on user-facing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a CRUD interface for the Project model so users can create, read, and delete their own projects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate server actions and UI components for CRUD operations on Project entity'

steps.title: Automated Testing and Debugging
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your logic, ensuring your application is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test suites for all server actions created in the previous step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write unit tests for Project service functions and verify against edge cases'

pro_tips: Always use the 'context' flag in Claude Code to limit the AI's scope to specific files, preventing hallucinated code paths.
Treat your Antigravity prompts like a Jira ticket: include the 'Why', 'What', and 'Acceptance Criteria' for better results.
Commit your code after every successful Claude Code block execution to maintain a granular, reversible version history.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature before pushing to production to catch security vulnerabilities in your auth logic.
When stuck, ask Claude Code to 'explain the current architectural bottleneck' to gain a senior engineer's perspective on your codebase.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, focus on deploying your app to Vercel or Railway and implementing a payment gateway like Stripe to complete the SaaS lifecycle.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live production environment. Stop waiting for permission to innovate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
